items.myth: Once features are selected in exploration, the same list is always optimal in production.
items.reality: Data drift, schema changes, and new cohorts can make yesterday's subset suboptimal; retrain selectors on schedule and monitor support-mask stability.
items.example: A COVID-era mobility feature ranked top in 2020 may be dropped by MI after behavior normalizes in 2024 retraining.

items.myth: Putting SelectKBest in a Pipeline automatically makes every workflow leakage-safe.
items.reality: Pipelines prevent leakage only if you never fit the selector on data that includes validation/test labels and you do not tune hyperparameters on the test set.
items.example: Calling `pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)` then `pipe.predict(X_test)` is safe; calling `sel.fit(X_all, y_all)` before split is not—even if a Pipeline exists elsewhere.

items.myth: Feature selection in engineering is the same topic as PCA-based reduction.
items.reality: Selection keeps original columns for pipelines and compliance; PCA creates new components—see the dimensionality-reduction track for when projection beats subsetting.
items.example: A bank auditor needs column names 'debt_to_income' and 'months_employed', not 'PC1' and 'PC2'.
